options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
How many lines to clear to win?
Number of checks will be this x2.5 this amount with tetrominos, x2 with pentominos, more with smaller shape types.

options.name: starting_shapes
options.description: Number of shapes to start with

options.name: extra_shapes
options.description: Extra shapes to add to the pool. This, as well as Line Clear Leniency, makes goaling easier/safer.

options.name: randomize_orientations
options.description: Randomize the starting orientation of pieces? If disabled, pieces are oriented with flat side down.

options.name: monomino_weight
options.description: Ratio of monominos (1-block) to all shape types

options.name: domino_weight
options.description: Ratio of dominos (2-blocks) to all shape types

options.name: tromino_weight
options.description: Ratio of tromino (3-blocks) to all shape types

options.name: tetromino_weight
options.description: Ratio of tetromino (4-blocks) to all shape types

options.name: pentomino_weight
options.description: Ratio of pentomino (5-blocks) to all shape types

options.name: starting_drop_method
options.description: How will shapes fall initially?

options.name: early_second_drop
options.description:
Guarantee second drop ability to be in Sphere 1?

When Starting Drop Method is Gravity, the game could potentially be slow-going if you don't get another
drop ability soon. This should help solve that.

options.name: early_rotate
options.description:
Guarantee one of your rotate abilities to be in Sphere 1?

Unnecessary for small/solo games since one will tend to be early anyway.

Better for large async games.

options.name: next_piece_slots
options.description: Number of Next Piece Slots present in the multiworld

options.name: hold_slots
options.description: Number of Hold Slots present in the multiworld

options.name: ability_whitelist
options.description:
Abilities that are allowed to be in the item pool

Valid Abilities: 
"Hard Drop", "Rotate Counterclockwise", "Gravity", "Soft Drop", "Lock Delay", "Kick", "Rotate Clockwise", "Ghost Piece"

options.name: trap_weight
options.description: Ratio of traps to all shapes

options.name: tutorials
options.description: How many times do you need to see the tutorial?

options.name: line_clear_leniency
options.description:
How many lines worth of extra blocks are needed for line clears to be considered in logic.

If zero, lines require the exact number of blocks it takes to clear them and all lines before.
This is not as strict as it sounds since stacking and clearing higher lines out-of-logic is still possible.

If you rely on trackers, it's recommended to set higher than zero.

options.name: max_stacking_height
options.description:
How high logic expects you to stack blocks to reach coins. This is NOT relevant to line clear locations.

If set to board height (currently 20), what's possible is in logic. Setting it lower makes logic more lenient.
If you rely on trackers, it's recommended to set it lower otherwise it will create unneeded pressure.

Warning! Setting this higher than your board height can make the game more difficult or even impossible!
Only do this if you accept the risk and want to challenge yourself in a solo game.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: death_on_restart
options.description: When Death Link is enabled, send a death whenever you reset your board

options.name: trap_link
options.description: When you receive a trap, everyone who enabled trap link receives a trap. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: energy_link
options.description: Allow contributing to the Energy Link pool by clearing blocks

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
